remember: Explain the concept of Fundamental Rights in the Indian Constitution.
List the key provisions of the Fundamental Rights in Part III of the Indian Constitution.
Identify and explain the differences between Fundamental Rights and Directive Principles of State Policy.
Describe the role of the Supreme Court in protecting and promoting Fundamental Rights.
Recall the significance of the 44th Amendment Act, 1978, on Fundamental Rights.
understand: Explain the concept of equality and how it is reflected in the Fundamental Rights.
Discuss the limitations of Fundamental Rights and how they can be restricted.
Analyze the impact of Fundamental Rights on the lives of citizens in a democratic society.
Explain the importance of freedom of speech and expression in a democratic setup.
Describe the relationship between Fundamental Rights and Directive Principles of State Policy.
apply: Apply the principles of Fundamental Rights to a real-life scenario, such as a court case.
Use the concept of equality to analyze a social issue or a policy.
Explain how the limitations of Fundamental Rights can be balanced with the need for social order.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the Supreme Court in protecting and promoting Fundamental Rights.
Develop a plan to promote and protect Fundamental Rights in a local community.
analyze: Analyze the historical context in which the Fundamental Rights were enshrined in the Indian Constitution.
Explain the differences between the original and amended versions of the Fundamental Rights.
Evaluate the impact of the 44th Amendment Act, 1978, on the Fundamental Rights.
Analyze the relationship between Fundamental Rights and the economic development of the country.
Discuss the tension between individual rights and collective rights in the context of Fundamental Rights.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT objective 1: Explain the concept of Fundamental Rights in the Indian Constitution and its significance.
NCERT objective 2: Describe the key provisions of the Fundamental Rights in Part III of the Indian Constitution.
NCERT objective 3: Analyze the relationship between Fundamental Rights and Directive Principles of State Policy.
board_exam_objectives: The students will be able to explain the concept of Fundamental Rights and its significance (CBSE Class 11 Political Science - Chapter 3).
The students will be able to describe the key provisions of the Fundamental Rights in Part III of the Indian Constitution (CBSE Class 11 Political Science - Chapter 3).
The students will be able to analyze the impact of Fundamental Rights on the lives of citizens in a democratic society (CBSE Class 11 Political Science - Chapter 3).
